auth.feature2: Smart space allocation as you upload

errors.passwordLength: Password must be at least 8 characters.

home.title: Home
home.subtitle: Cloud accounts
home.heroTitle: Connect accounts and view all files like a Drive interface.
home.heroDesc: Sync metadata from real cloud accounts and display file list in a Google Drive-like interface.

hub.noAccounts: No connected accounts yet. Connect a provider from the Storage page.
hub.noFiles: No files yet across your connected providers.

drive.title: My Drive
drive.starredSubtitle: Files marked with a star from all supported providers.
drive.dragDropTitle: Drag, drop, and stream directly to the target cloud
drive.dragDropDesc: File payloads pass through the Node.js proxy, while metadata is mirrored locally and progress is reported by WebSocket.
drive.deleteConfirmDesc: Item will be moved to trash.
drive.metadataDescription: Metadata from provider and local mirror.

quota.title: Storage
quota.disconnectConfirmDesc: Account will be removed from CloudHub but data in the original cloud remains safe.

pcloud.hint: Use your pCloud email and password.

yandex.title: Connect Yandex Disk
yandex.hint: You will be redirected to Yandex to authorize access.

s3.title: Connect S3 Storage
s3.hint: Works with any S3-compatible service (Cloudflare R2, Backblaze B2, Tebi.io, Storj, iDrive e2, MinIO, and more). Enter your access keys and endpoint from the provider console.
s3.label: Display Name

help.title: CloudHub Quick Help
help.subtitle: A brief summary to help you understand CloudHub's main workflow faster.
help.createFolderDesc: Use the New button to create a new folder directly from active storage.
help.uploadFilesDesc: Upload files or folders and monitor progress through the app's process notifications.
help.searchFilesDesc: Use the search box at the top to browse files in CloudHub.
usageTipsList: Use the sidebar menu to switch between home, drive, and storage overview.
Click the CloudHub logo on desktop to open the creator profile and open source project info.
Press the Esc button to close any open dialog.
help.connectAccount: How to connect a cloud account
connectAccountSteps: Open the Storage page, then press the Connect button to select a provider like Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, or MEGA.
Then follow the login or authorization process according to the provider. If the account is disconnected, suspended, or the token is no longer valid, use the Connect button to try reconnecting.
After successfully connected, the storage capacity and files from that account will automatically appear in CloudHub.
help.howItWorksStep1: You connect multiple cloud accounts from various providers into one CloudHub dashboard.
help.howItWorksStep2: The app will sync file metadata and storage capacity so all accounts can be viewed from one place.
help.howItWorksStep3: When you create a folder, upload, rename, or delete, CloudHub will forward that action to the related provider and display the progress in the interface.
help.howItWorksStep4: This way, CloudHub serves as a unified management layer for many cloud accounts in a neater experience.

profile.title: Credits
profile.subtitle: About the creator of CloudHub
profile.letsConnectDesc: Follow my journey, works, and latest activities through the following official channels

storage.title: Storage
storage.subtitle: View all cloud accounts, their quotas, then connect or disconnect accounts.
storage.reconnectNotSupported: Provider {provider} does not support automatic reconnect.

allocation.title: Storage allocation
allocation.subtitle: Choose how new uploads are distributed across your connected accounts.
allocation.reorderHint: Drag accounts to set the upload priority. Newly connected accounts go to the end.
allocation.noAccounts: Connect at least one account to configure allocation.

strategies.round_robin_desc: Uploads take turns across accounts in order, regardless of capacity.
strategies.weighted_round_robin_desc: Larger accounts receive proportionally more uploads based on capacity.
strategies.least_used_desc: Sends each upload to the account with the lowest used percentage.
strategies.most_free_desc: Sends each upload to the account with the most free space. Best for large files.
strategies.manual_desc: Fills accounts in the exact order you set below.

preview.notAvailable: Preview is not available for this file type.
preview.failed: Preview failed to load. Some provider files are not compatible with lightweight preview.
